summary: End-to-end test of Bhamora Foundation Next.js+FastAPI rebuild. Backend: 11/11 pytest cases passed (health, newsletter idempotency, contact, volunteer, 422 validation). Frontend: home/navbar/impact-stats/programs/gallery+lightbox+awards/volunteer 3-step form/donate page/contact form/footer newsletter/about/impact/privacy/terms/mobile-menu all render and integrate with backend APIs correctly. MongoDB persistence confirmed (newsletter_subscribers, contact_messages, volunteer_applications). One minor data-testid naming inconsistency on gallery filter button (women vs women-empowerment) is documented below.

ui_bugs.issue: Spec expected data-testid 'gallery-filter-women-empowerment' but rendered testid is 'gallery-filter-women' (category id is 'women' in /app/src/content/galleryData.ts). Functionality works; only the testid string differs from the spec.

action_items: (Optional) Rename galleryCategories id 'women' to 'women-empowerment' in /app/src/content/galleryData.ts to match the spec testid 'gallery-filter-women-empowerment'. Pure naming fix; no functional impact.

(Cosmetic) AdSense Next script emits console warning 'AdSense head tag doesn't support data-nscript attribute'. Per the review note this is not critical, but could be silenced by moving AdSense out of the <head> Script or using strategy='lazyOnload'.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py is clean, async, env-driven, uses EmailStr/Pydantic validation, and excludes Mongo _id from responses (only returns success/message dicts).
CORS is wide-open (allow_origins=['*']). Acceptable for a public NGO site, but consider tightening to the production domain for the contact/newsletter/volunteer write endpoints.
Newsletter endpoint correctly handles idempotent re-subscribe (returns already_subscribed=true).
Volunteer/Contact endpoints have no rate-limiting or captcha — could be abused. Recommend adding a simple rate limit (e.g., slowapi) or a honeypot field before public launch.
Frontend uses next/font (Fraunces + Manrope) and respects prefers-reduced-motion in Reveal. Looks good.
Counters animate correctly on scroll into view (Impact section shows 5,000+ / 700+ / 25,000+ / 600+ as specified).

success_rate.frontend: ~98% (all flows verified; 1 minor testid naming mismatch on gallery filter)

test_credentials: None required — site has no auth flows.
seed_data_creation: Test rows inserted via API into newsletter_subscribers, contact_messages, volunteer_applications collections (all prefixed with TEST_ in email/name where applicable).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend at https://301129e4-3cf9-47fd-bdcf-8a1e4795f52a.preview.emergentagent.com/api/* is fully working (health, newsletter, contact, volunteer). MongoDB is bhamora_foundation. Frontend Next.js 15 App Router at /app with frontend symlink to /app. Reusable regression suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. Gallery filter for Women Empowerment uses testid 'gallery-filter-women' (not 'gallery-filter-women-empowerment'). All key UI elements have data-testid attributes; multi-step volunteer form, donate amount/preview, lightbox open+Escape close, and newsletter idempotency were all verified end-to-end against MongoDB persistence.
rca of the issue: Gallery filter testid mismatch RCA: /app/src/content/galleryData.ts defines galleryCategories with id: 'women' for Women Empowerment. The gallery page renders data-testid={`gallery-filter-${c.id}`} → 'gallery-filter-women'. The review spec listed 'gallery-filter-women-empowerment'. Fix: change id to 'women-empowerment' in galleryData.ts (galleryItems referencing category 'women' should be updated too if any).
